title: Tamil Nadu Government Formation LIVE: IUML Says Won't Support Vijay, AMMK Wants AIADMK To Form Government
content: Tamil Nadu Election Results 2026 Live Updates: There is buzz that the Dravidian arch-rivals DMK and AIADMK may come together after over 50 years to form a government and keep Vijay out of power.

title: Votes, Victory and Viral Interest: Vijay-TVK Ride High on Google Trends
content: Tamilaga Vettri Kazhagam has secured a majority in the Tamil Nadu Assembly elections, a victory widely attributed to the star appeal of actor-turned-politician Vijay.

title: Opinion: Opinion | No Permanent Rivals In Politics As Vijay Turns Tamil Nadu Into 3-Way Battle
content: Back in 2006, when former Chief Minister J Jayalalithaa formed an alliance with MDMK chief Vaiko, she told waiting reporters that "there are no permanent friends or allies in politics."

title: Toddler Mauled To Death By Stray Dogs In Rajasthan's Jhalawar
content: The toddler, identified as Gauri, was allegedly picked up by a pack of seven to eight stray dogs and dragged nearly 50 metres away before being mauled.

title: Canal Worth Rs 2 Crore Demolished In Gujarat After MLA's Hammer Test Fails
content: The Mahi Irrigation Minor Canal in Vanthvali village, intended to serve local farmers, is now being completely demolished by JCB machines following an intervention by the local MLA.

title: Pics: US Releases Fresh UFO Evidence After Years Of Secrecy
content: The Pentagon has started releasing a fresh set of files linked to Unidentified flying objects (UFO), or what the US government officially calls "unidentified anomalous phenomena" (UAPs), saying the public should be allowed to draw its own conclusions

title: China Admits It Sent Experts To Pakistani Air Bases During Operation Sindoor
content: This is the first time China has admitted that its personnel gave on-ground technical support to their Pakistani allies.

title: Ahmedabad Air India Crash Probe In Final Stages, Report Likely In A Month: Minister
content: New Delhi, May 8 (IANS) Union Civil Aviation Minister Ram Mohan Naidu on Friday said that the investigation into the crash of Air India Flight AI 171, which claimed 260 lives in Ahmedabad, is in its final stages and is likely to be completed within a

title: Big Relief For Commuters As New Train Between Delhi And Faridabad Gets Nod
content: Delhi Chief Minister Rekha Gupta said the service is expected to ease travel for people commuting daily between Faridabad and Delhi for work.

title: 3 Men Drown In Jhelum In J&K's Bandipora, Omar Abdullah Expresses Grief
content: The incident took place at Chandergir area of the north Kashmir district when the three youths were washing tents in the river, officials said.

title: Opinion: Blog | Suvendu Adhikari: The Man Who Waited For 15 Years - And Won
content: The story of Suvendu Adhikari begins in 2011 - from a stage in Dharmatala that made him invisible and denied him the limelight he thought should've been his.

title: PM Modi To Lead Somnath Amrit Parv Celebrations, Grand Roadshow Planned
content: PM Modi will lead a 1-km roadshow from the Triveni Helipad to the statue of Veer Hamirji Gohil, featuring cultural dances from various Indian states

title: Will Fulfil All Promises Made In BJP Election Manifesto: Suvendu Adhikari
content: "Bhoi is out and, as Prime Minister Narendra Modi ji has said, bharosa is in," Suvendu Adhikari said amid loud cheers from party MLAs and supporters.

title: Tanker Carrying Chinese Crew Attacked In Strait Of Hormuz: Beijing
content: There are Chinese nationals aboard the vessel, but no reported crew casualties so far, foreign ministry spokesperson Lin Jian said.

title: NASA's Webb Telescope Captures Clearest Picture Yet Of Exoplanet Surface
content: NASA's James Webb Space Telescope gathered data on a rocky exoplanet with a diameter about 30% larger than Earth - called LHS 3844 b, or Kua'kua.

title: How A Collector In Madhya Pradesh's Damoh Set Trap, Exposed Espionage Ring
content: The sensational discovery emerged after confidential details of a planned surprise inspection were mysteriously leaked to concerned officials within minutes before anyone had even stepped out of the Collector's room.

title: New Suspected Hantavirus Case Identified On Remote South Atlantic Island
content: The World Health Organisation said it would provide an update on the latest suspected and confirmed case numbers later on Friday.

title: West Bengal Government Formation LIVE: Suvendu Adhikari Named Next Chief Minister Of Bengal
content: West Bengal Government Formation LIVE Updates: Prime Minister Narendra Modi, Union Home Minister Amit Shah, BJP president Nitin Nabin, along with several Union ministers, are expected to attend the swearing-in ceremony.

title: Op Sindoor: How close India and Pakistan came to the brink one year ago
content: Following a deadly terror attack in Kashmir, India launched precision strikes on militant camps in Pakistan, escalating tensions to a full military standoff. Despite Pakistan's nuclear threats, India maintained a strong stance, ultimately leading to a ceasefire after significant damage to Pakistani military assets.

title: Will rupee continue its free fall against US dollar or recover?
content: The Indian rupee is facing significant pressure, weakening against the US dollar due to soaring crude oil prices driven by Middle East geopolitical tensions and persistent foreign fund outflows. Despite India's robust economic growth, the currency's slide continues as the Reserve Bank of India intervenes to curb volatility rather than target a specific exchange rate.

title: After 43 yrs, US family sells electrical firm for $1.7bn; gives 540 workers $240m
content: A US-based businessman, Graham Walker, gifted his 540 employees a staggering $240 million from the $1.7 billion sale of his family's company, Fibrebond Corp. This generous act, where workers received an average of $443,000, stemmed from a condition Walker imposed before the sale to power-management giant Eaton.

title: 'Regardless of jersey colour': Kohli's pep talk after duck goes viral - Watch
content: Even after the defeat, RCB stayed in the top four. However, they still need better performances in their remaining four matches.

title: 'You have 10 working days to voluntarily recognise unions': Google UK staff tells company
content: Google's DeepMind employees are demanding union recognition amid concerns over their AI technology's use by the US and Israeli militaries. Workers seek a commitment against developing AI weapons and surveillance tools, urging management to voluntarily recognize their unions or face legal action. The move highlights growing anxieties about AI's ethical implications and its potential for harm.

title: Happy Mother’s Day 2026: Top 50 wishes, quotes and messages to share
content: This Mother's Day, move beyond generic greetings to honor the women who raised you with meaningful messages. The article offers categorized heartfelt, witty, and minimalist quotes, acknowledging the "mental load" and celebrating "mothers of choice."

title: Decoding Bengal SIR data: Of 123 margins, 49 in closer focus
content: West Bengal's election saw a political earthquake, with BJP's massive win overshadowing concerns about Special Intensive Revision (SIR). Analysis reveals voter deletions exceeded winning margins in numerous seats, particularly in areas with significant supplementary deletions. While some seats showed clear BJP gains, others indicated TMC erosion without a direct BJP benefit, highlighting complex local dynamics.

title: After $6.25bn 'gift' to Trump Accounts, Michael & Susan Dell donate $750m to Texas University
content: Michael and Susan Dell are donating $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin to establish a new AI-focused medical center and research campus. This significant philanthropic gift, named UT Dell Medical Center, will integrate medicine, science, and computing, aiming to revolutionize healthcare through artificial intelligence.

title: India’s forex reserves fall by $7.79 billion to $690.69 billion
content: India's foreign exchange reserves experienced a significant drop of $7.794 billion, reaching $690.693 billion in the week ending May 1. This decline, primarily driven by a fall in foreign currency assets and gold reserves, follows a previous decrease. The central bank's intervention in the forex market to support the rupee is a key factor behind this trend.

title: Real Madrid fine Tchouameni and Valverde $588,000 each after training-ground fight
content: The club said no sporting punishment would be given to the two players and added that the fine “thereby concludes the internal procedures” started after the incident.

title: 'Currently in therapy': Simone Biles opens up on mental health journey
content: Simone Biles opens up about her mental health struggles following the Tokyo Olympics, reflecting on the pressure that led her to step away from competition. In a recent interview, the Olympic champion shared how the experience changed her perspective on strength and vulnerability. Two years later, Biles continues to focus on her well-being while keeping her future in gymnastics open, including a possible return for the 2028 Olympics in Los Angeles.

title: Mike Vrabel seemingly upset after Dianna Russini mentions husband
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini’s alleged affair rumors refuse to die down even as the two remain distant from it and continue to deny their inappropriate relationship with one another, despite having families of their own, sparking outrage. Days after their picture from a New York City bar in 2020 sparked chaos, a new clip of Mike Vrabel being disappointed as Dianna Russini speaks about her husband has raised eyebrows.

title: Chargers linked to risky $120M 49ers move for Justin Herbert
content: The Los Angeles Chargers have recorded a relatively impressive performance in the last few seasons with 11 wins and 6 losses in both 2024 and 2025, sparking hope among fans. While the team faced a brutal loss against the New England Patriots in the Wild Card round, all eyes are on the Los Angeles Chargers’ roster ahead of the new season.

title: Middle East conflict burns Indian oil firms: Rs 30k cr monthly hit to keep fuel prices stable
content: India's state-run oil companies are absorbing nearly Rs 30,000 crore in monthly losses to maintain stable petrol, diesel, and LPG prices amidst soaring global energy costs. This financial burden arises from crude oil price surges and supply disruptions in West Asia, with the government prioritizing consumer protection.

title: 'They don't hit sixes': Gavaskar slams Rahul and Nissanka approach against KKR
content: DC openers KL Rahul and Pathum Nissanka managed only two sixes in the first six overs, both of them coming from Nissanka. Rahul did not hit a six during his innings as DC scored 55 runs in the powerplay.

title: KL Rahul scripts history vs KKR, becomes first batter to...
content: KL Rahul etched his name in IPL history, becoming the first player to surpass 1000 runs for three different franchises.  He achieved this remarkable feat for Punjab Kings, Lucknow Super Giants, and now Delhi Capitals.  Rahul also set a new record for the quickest 1000 runs for Delhi, reaching the milestone in just 23 innings.

title: Moushumi Chatterjee on Rajesh Khanna’s downfall: Success went to his head
content: Moushumi Chatterjee reminisces about working with Rajesh Khanna, describing him as a "spoilt brat" whose superstardom affected him. She reflects philosophically on loneliness and karma, noting that everyone faces such phases. Chatterjee also recounts a past incident where Khanna questioned her daughter's paternity, to which she responded sharply, leaving him speechless.

title: Operation Sindoor: China admits it provided support to Pakistan last year
content: China has for the first time publicly acknowledged providing on-ground technical assistance to Pakistan during India's Operation Sindoor last year. Chinese engineers from AVIC's Chengdu Aircraft Design and Research Institute were involved in ensuring Pakistani aircraft operated at full combat capability. This marks the first official indication of direct Chinese personnel involvement in supporting Pakistani military operations during the conflict.

title: Hindutva mascot, ex-TMC insider: 5 reasons why BJP chose Suvendu Adhikari as Bengal CM
content: Suvendu Adhikari emerged as the BJP's choice for West Bengal Chief Minister after years of being the party's strongest anti-Mamata Banerjee face. His victories, including defeating Banerjee in her stronghold, solidified his position. Adhikari's insider knowledge of the TMC and ability to consolidate Hindu votes made him the natural leader for the BJP's first government in the state.

title: The Bourbon Supremacy: Kash Patel and the MAGA art of merchandising power
content: The important detail, of course, is that this was not just whiskey. It was Kash Patel whiskey, or at least whiskey transformed into a Kash Patel object, reportedly engraved with “Kash Patel FBI Director”, an FBI-style shield, an eagle, his signature and the number 9, presumably a reference to his place in the line of FBI directors.

title: US stocks rise toward record highs as strong jobs data boosts investor confidence
content: Wall Street stocks surged on Friday, with the S&P 500 and Nasdaq nearing record highs, buoyed by stronger-than-expected US jobs data. Despite ongoing Middle East tensions and oil price concerns, positive corporate earnings from companies like Monster Beverage and Akamai Technologies also fueled the rally, indicating economic resilience.

title: Trump’s ‘very interesting’ UFO files out: Pentagon releases over 160 ‘never-before-seen’ UAP records
content: The Trump administration has unveiled over 160 UAP-related files, photos, and videos, aiming for greater transparency on government knowledge of unexplained phenomena. This release, part of the PURSUE program, includes never-before-seen materials from lunar missions and FBI investigations.

title: Tamil Nadu: Left extends support to Vijay’s TVK, but to 'continue to work' with DMK
content: TVK has got the support of Congress (5 seats), CPI (2) and CPM (2). TVK is likely to cross the magic number of 118 as it is expecting a positive response from the VCK leader, whose party has two MLAs.

title: Hantavirus scare on cruise ship: ICMR says no immediate threat to India
content: Two Indian nationals aboard a cruise ship have tested positive for hantavirus, but health officials assure there's no immediate threat to India.  The virus, primarily spread by rodents, does not transmit easily between humans. Global health authorities are monitoring the situation, with the public risk deemed very low.

title: Gulf crisis: Govt says fuel supply, prices stable despite global oil market turmoil
content: India has successfully navigated the Middle East crisis, ensuring stable fuel supplies and uninterrupted maritime operations. Despite global price surges, consumers are shielded through excise duty cuts and increased domestic production. State-run oil companies are absorbing significant daily losses to maintain current prices, while Indian seafarers in the Gulf remain safe and maritime operations at Indian ports are normal.

title: Pooran grabs Krunal Pandya by collar after heated on-field exchange - Watch
content: Lucknow Super Giants posted a massive 209 for 3 in 19 overs. Nicholas Pooran contributed a brisk 38 off 23 balls. A tense on-field moment provided a late flourish with an unbeaten 32 off just 10 deliveries. Krunal Pandya dismissed Arshin Kulkarni to break the opening partnership. Royal Challengers Bangalore faced a daunting revised chase in the rain-affected encounter.

title: India’s job market decoded: Where Indians work, what they earn — Explained in charts
content: India's labor market is shifting from agriculture to non-farm jobs, yet agriculture remains a significant employer. While overall labor force participation is steady, stark gender, caste, and geographic inequalities persist, impacting access to stable, better-paying work. Informality continues to dominate, with a substantial portion of casual workers earning below minimum wages.

title: Suvendu Adhikari: Ex-Mamata loyalist, who defeated her twice, to be Bengal's 1st BJP CM
content: Suvendu Adhikari comes from one of the state’s most influential political families. His father, Sisir Adhikari, served both in the West Bengal Assembly and the Lok Sabha, while his brother Dibyendu Adhikari was earlier a Trinamool Congress (TMC) MP before joining the BJP in 2024. Suvendu began his political journey with the Congress during the 1995 municipal polls before moving to the newly formed TMC alongside his father in 1998. He rose rapidly within Mamata Banerjee’s party and became one of its strongest organisational faces in coastal Bengal.

title: 'Never a profitable scheme': Pvt hosps shut door on Mamata's health insurance scheme
content: Private hospitals in Kolkata are refusing or restricting admissions for Swasthya Sathi beneficiaries as they await clarity on the scheme's future under the new government. Hospitals cite concerns over the scheme's profitability and are unofficially suspending admissions, impacting patients seeking treatment.

title: ‘Bowlers bowling with one hand tied’: Gavaskar sounds alarm over T20 imbalance
content: Cricket legend Sunil Gavaskar is concerned about the game's increasing tilt towards batters, especially in T20s. He highlights shorter boundaries, fielding restrictions, and powerful bats as key issues. Gavaskar specifically advocates for a more lenient wide bouncer rule, arguing bowlers are unfairly penalized. He urges the ICC Cricket Committee to consider measures to restore balance for the bowling fraternity.

title: Soha Ali Khan says Sharmila Tagore’s cancer brought family closer
content: Soha Ali Khan recently shared insights into her mother Sharmila Tagore's stage one lung cancer diagnosis. The experience prompted a shift towards more open communication within their family, as Sharmila Tagore herself recognized the need for greater emotional expression after reading a book on the subject.

title: Gold price today: Yellow metal jumps; check 24K, 22K city-wise rates
content: Gold prices surged on Friday, driven by robust spot demand and fresh speculative buying.  Across major Indian cities, retail rates for 22K, 24K, and 18K gold saw an uptick.  Futures contracts for August delivery on MCX climbed, mirroring a stronger global sentiment for the precious metal.  This rally reflects increased trader confidence and positive market momentum.

title: NFL fans stunned by fresh Dianna Russini-Mike Vrabel accusations
content: Mike Vrabel and Dianna Russini are facing another wave of backlash after reports claimed the two spent private time together while Russini was pregnant with her first child in 2021. Former NFL player Breiden Fehoko slammed both publicly, questioning their behavior and fueling intense online speculation. NBC analyst Chris Simms also reacted, saying the controversy crossed a new line because of the pregnancy angle. Neither Russini nor Vrabel has publicly responded to the latest claims.

title: ‘Enough is enough’: SC raps MP govt over delay in action against min over remarks on Col Qureshi
content: The Supreme Court has strongly criticized the Madhya Pradesh government for delaying the decision on sanctioning prosecution for minister Kunwar Vijay Shah's "most unfortunate" remarks against Army officer Sofiya Qureshi. The court has ordered the state to comply with its earlier directive within four weeks, emphasizing that "enough is enough."

title: SC takes cognisance of resolution about HC judge sending advocate to custody
content: The SCBA had urged Chief Justice of India Surya Kant, in a resolution passed on May 6, to look into the incident while expressing shock at the judge’s reported conduct

title: Plea in Supreme Court says Governor is ‘duty-bound’ to invite TVK to form government in Tamil Nadu
content: The petition, filed by advocate A. Lakshminarayanan, said such a move by the Governor would “sound the death-knell for democracy” and be against the mandate of the electorate

title: Census enumerators ID can be verified through QR code
content: Lalit Jain, Director of Census Operations (DCO), Haryana seeks to allay concerns among residents in high-rise apartment complexes that are refusing to allow entry to enumerators

title: West Bengal government formation LIVE: Suvendu Adhikari meets Bengal Governor, stakes claim to form government
content: Union Home Minister Amit Shah, who presided over BJP legislature party meeting said that all the proposals were received in favour of Suvendu Adhikari.

title: TCS case: Nida Khan sent to police custody till May 11; AIMIM corporator booked for harbouring her
content: The police have also booked an AIMIM corporator from Chhatrapati Sambhajinagar on the charges of allegedly harbouring Ms. Khan while she absconded from the law

title: Educational institutions must ensure bus safety compliance by May 20, warn officials
content: Inspections of buses operated by educational institutions began on May 1 and will cover 1,571 buses across the NTR district

title: SC criticises Punjab govt. for inability to curb drug menace
content: Drug abuse and trafficking continue galore as only small peddlers were caught while none dared to lay a hand on the big fish who ran the racket, says CJI-led Bench

title: Karnataka among top three States in corruption: NCRB
content: The State has the most number of Disproportionate Assets cases at 75, followed by Odisha with 62 and Assam with 44 cases

title: CPI and CPI(M) tell Tamil Nadu Governor their support is for stable secular government
content: The letter was sent under the instruction of the party's State secretary, M. Veerapandian and the legislature party leader T. Ramachandran

title: ‘Extremely shy’ to direct arrest as first option, no need to sensationalise probe: SC in Anil Ambani’s ADAG ‘bank fraud’ case
content: CBI report estimates loss to public sector banks and LIC at over ₹27,000 crore in seven cases involving Anil Ambani’s ADAG and its firms. Top court says arrest must be last resort, and not first option.

title: Prosecution failed to establish ‘conspiracy’ to kill Sohrabuddin Sheikh: Bombay HC
content: The court said that not a single witness identified the accused in the dock as the persons who had abducted Sohrabuddin Sheikh, Kausar Bi and Tulsiram Prajapati

title: Assam elections 2026: How have smaller allies fared?
content: The Opposition’s ‘three Gogoi’ formula to counter the BJP failed as both Congress state chief Gaurav Gogoi and AJP chief Gogoi lost from their respective seats in Jorhat and Khowang.

title: DRDO, IAF successfully conduct maiden trial of indigenous TARA glide weapon
content: TARA is India’s first indigenous glide weapon system capable of converting conventional unguided warheads into precision-guided weapons for accurately engaging ground-based targets

title: MGNREGS coverage and workdays fell sharply in 2025–26: LibTech India
content: Despite a rise in registered households, employment generation dropped across most States, leading  to an average income loss of ₹1,221 for each household; 40% fewer households completed 100 days of work

title: Jockeying for Congress leadership erupts into open, manifesting as competing marches and vying poster campaigns
content: The three-way leadership tussle spilt onto the streets across Kerala, manifesting as competing rallies by Congress workers professing their respective factional loyalties.  Amid the discord, Satheesan urges cadres to refrain from group demonstrations and public declarations of loyalty.

title: Suvendu Adhikari to become the first BJP Chief Minister of West Bengal
content: Chief Minister-elect Suvendu Adhikari reached Kolkata Lok Bhavan and met Governor R.N. Ravi to stake claim to form the government. He will take oath as the Chief Minister of West Bengal at Kolkata’s Brigade Parade Grounds on Saturday

title: Congress slams Modi Government over women’s safety citing NCRB data
content: Mallikarjun Kharge said NCRB data pointed that cybercrime had surged by 1,689% and 10,546 farmers, 52,931 daily wage labourers and 14,488 students died by suicide in 2024

title: Manipur BJP spokesperson quits party over ongoing ethnic crisis
content: The paramilitary Assam Rifles denies the accusation that it failed to protect Naga villagers along the India-Myanmar border from external aggression

title: NCC cadets test shooting skills at Combined Annual Training Camp in Tirupati
content: The 10-day camp at Alipiri, which began on May 5 and ends on May 14, includes drill, firing, obstacle course, debate, sports and cultural programmes

title: Niti Aayog flags poor student retention, learning outcomes in report
content: Niti Aayog report recommends ‘Cylindrical’ Schooling to fix the “leaky pipeline” of student dropouts, which involves creating composite schools that cover Grades 1-12 under one roof

title: Watch: Vijay meets Governor Arlekar for third time after CPI, CPI(M) support | Above the Fold | 08.05.2026
content: From Tamil Nadu’s dramatic government formation talks and China’s fresh remarks on Operation Sindoor to West Bengal getting its first BJP Chief Minister - Top stories of the day.

title: Class 12 State Board exams: Tamil Nadu registers 95.2% pass rate
content: Erode district recorded the highest pass percentage of 98.8, while Sivaganga district stood second with 98.05% pass, as per the data

title: Chandrababu Naidu calls for full utilisation of Central funds
content: He directs departments to spend an average of ₹8,000 crore every quarter and aggressively pursue additional assistance from the Union government

title: T.N. Government formation: IUML endorses M.K. Stalin’s demand for a new government
content: Professor Kader Mohideen, the leader of the party, said his party has not given a letter of support to Tamilaga Vetri Kazhagam, led by actor Vijay.

title: Watermelon yields good turnover for Kudumbashree women farmers
content: Kudumbashree’s women farmers in Kerala have reported a remarkable turnover of ₹6.78 crore from watermelon cultivation on 758 acres as part of the ‘Venal Madhuram’ initiative this year, nearly doubling last year’s earnings.

title: Kerala filmmaker John Abraham’s cult classic Amma Ariyan’s restored version to premiere at Cannes
content: The film with a cult following, restored by the not-for-profit organisation Film Heritage Foundation, is the only Indian feature film selected for world premiere at the festival this year. The premiere is scheduled on May 16

title: Calcutta HC to hear pleas against Great Nicobar project in June; overrules Centre’s objections
content: HC dismisses Centre’s claim that petitioner has no locus standi, noting that she is espousing the cause of vulnerable tribals; says project of “national importance” and high cost must still follow the law

title: Former IPS officer’s wife found dead at Jubilee Hills residence in Hyderabad, Nepal gang role suspected
content: Preliminary findings suggest that the gang gained entry into the house with the alleged assistance of a woman domestic help

title: Caterers in Kerala hit the streets as LPG price hike triggers crisis
content: They stage protests in all 14 districts demanding the rollback of the hike of ₹993 per cylinder, which came into effect on May 1

content: The TOI Science Desk stands as an inquisitive team of journalists, ceaselessly delving into the realms of discovery to curate a captivating collection of news, features, and articles from the vast and ever-evolving world of science for the readers of The Times of India. Consider us your scientific companion, delivering a daily dose of wonder and enlightenment. Whether it's the intricacies of genetic engineering, the marvels of space exploration, or the latest in artificial intelligence, the TOI Science Desk ensures you stay connected to the pulse of the scientific world. At the TOI Science Desk, we are not just reporters; we are storytellers of scientific narratives. We are committed to demystifying the intricacies of science, making it accessible and engaging for readers of all backgrounds. Join us as we craft knowledge with precision and passion, bringing you on a journey where the mysteries of the universe unfold with every word. Read More

title: Glow hard: Trump's nuclear threat grows darker in face of Iranian defiance
content: both sides exchanged fire in the Strait of Hormuz on friday amid a diplomatic impasse . the global community appeared to be coming to a realization that Washington may have overestimated its hand in the conflict . signs of Iranian resilience and defiance prompted Trump to issue what was seen as another veiled nuclear threat .
